pages.raw_text:
The unions have shown a COM
mendable spirit in indorsing the
program of the park board to issue
million dollars of bonds to nyo-
vide Spokane with a better system
of parks, and to beautify the city
generally. When the matter was
brought up before the central
council on a previous occasion it
was not Endorsed because, from the
manner in which it was brought
before the labor men, an indorse.
ment would have placed them I'M
der obligations to take the initia-
tive in the matter.


On last Monday night. however.
the park board asked only for an
Endorsement of its program, which
the delegates to the council cheer
fully gave, and expressed their will.
ingness to cooperate in securing
the bond issue. The union mel
know that few real estate specu-
lators will profit if the park i'm
provements are made but they
have given the proposition a larger
consideration. They have not al
lowed the temporary profit of few
real estate spectators to hinder
them from taking an action which
will result in health and enjoyment
for workingmen and their families
for years to come.


OFFICE SUITE FOR TYPOS.


The Spokane Falls Typographical
union, No. 1913, has secured an Of
flee suite in the Wolverton block
Although the meetings of the local
will still be held at the old head
quarters at the Carpenters hall, the
office in the Wolverton will be the
official headquarters of the union.
w. d. Wheaton, who was elected
secretary and placed on a salary
by the typos on last Sunday, will
be found at the new headquarters,
where he will carry on the business
of the local
